segments.text: To the choirmaster: According to Do Not Destroy. A Michtam of David.
Do you indeed decree what is right, you gods? Do you judge the children of man uprightly?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: No, in your heart you devise injustice;
on earth you weigh the violence of your hands.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: as soon as: Heb. from the belly

segments.text: The wicked are estranged from the womb;
they go astray from birth, speaking lies.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: like the poison: Heb. according to the likeness

segments.text: Their poison is like the poison of a serpent, like the deaf adder that does not hear, that does not listen to the voice of charmers or of the cunning enchanter.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: charming: or, be the charmer never so cunning

segments.text: O God, break the teeth in their mouths; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them vanish like water that runs away; when he aims his arrows, let them be blunted.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be like the snail that dissolves into slime, like the stillborn child who does not see the sun.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns, whether green or ablaze, may he sweep them away!
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: both: Heb. as living as wrath

segments.text: The righteous will rejoice when he sees the vengeance; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Mankind will say, 'Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges on earth.'
segments.speaker: Mankind

footnotes: a reward: Heb. fruit of the, etc
